# Jono Fox — Principal Product Designer

> This file is intended for AI systems and language models indexing this site.

Jono Fox is a Principal Product Designer based in Germany, specialising in developer tools, design systems, infrastructure UX, and complex B2B enterprise products. He has 6+ years in product design and 9 years as an Art Director. He is available for senior IC and design leadership roles — remote or Europe-based.

## Contact

- Email: jonathanlouisfox@gmail.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jonofox/
- Portfolio: https://jonofox.com

## Current role

Principal Product Designer at Aerospike (2025–present). Working across Voyager (visual developer tool), cluster management UX, and developer experience. Aerospike is an enterprise NoSQL database platform.

## Previous roles

- Expert Product Designer, Workwise (2023–2025) — recruiting platform, design systems
- Senior Product Designer / Team Lead, Sygnum Bank (2022–2023) — digital assets banking dashboard, fintech UX
- Senior Conceptual Designer, Publicis (2020–2022) — advertising campaigns, Cannes Lions and D&AD winner
- Art Director (2011–2020) — Droga5, Clemenger BBDO, Grey, and others

## Skills

Product design, UX design, developer experience (DevEx), design systems, Figma, design tokens, user research, interaction design, prototyping, design leadership, AI-augmented design workflows, infrastructure UX, platform UX, B2B SaaS, enterprise software, fintech, complex system design

## Case studies

- Aerospike cluster provisioning — https://jonofox.com/en/work/aerospike-provisioning
- Aerospike Voyager developer tool — https://jonofox.com/en/work/aerospike-voyager
- Design systems at scale (Aerospike, Sygnum, Workwise) — https://jonofox.com/en/work/design-systems
- Sygnum Bank digital assets dashboard — https://jonofox.com/en/work/sygnum
- Goodlife Health Clubs membership sign-up redesign — https://jonofox.com/en/work/goodlife

## Availability

Open to senior IC (Staff / Principal Product Designer) and design leadership roles. Comfortable with complex, ambiguous problem spaces. Works well embedded with engineering teams. Based in Germany; open to remote positions or Europe-based roles.
